data.title: record-review.sh: reviewer-findings.json artifact written to wrong dir -> hash mismatch when artifacts dirs differ
data.description:
## Incident Overview
When a code-reviewer sub-agent writes reviewer-findings.json to a relative path (.claude/artifacts/) while the session's actual artifacts dir is at an absolute path (/tmp/workflow-plugin-XXXX/), record-review.sh cannot find the matching file, reports a hash mismatch, and blocks the commit.

## Root Cause
get_artifacts_dir() in deps.sh returns different paths depending on whether WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR is set and whether the caller is in the worktree or the main session. The orchestrator passes WORKFLOW_PLUGIN_ARTIFACTS_DIR to the sub-agent, but if the sub-agent resolves it to a different absolute path, the file ends up in the wrong location.

## Expected vs Observed
- **Expected**: record-review.sh finds reviewer-findings.json in the same artifacts dir that the session's review gate reads from. The file is written to and read from the same path.
- **Observed**: reviewer-findings.json written to .claude/artifacts/ but record-review.sh reads from /tmp/workflow-plugin-XXXX/, causing hash mismatch and blocking commit.

## Workaround Applied
Manually copy reviewer-findings.json to the correct artifacts dir before calling record-review.sh.

## Affected Component
plugins/dso/hooks/record-review.sh and plugins/dso/hooks/lib/deps.sh — artifacts dir resolution consistency
